channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died of complications from diabetes, says NYC medical examiner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died as a result of complications from diabetes, New York City's medical examiner said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tariffs will cost families nearly $5k per year, Kentucky governor says
item.description: "That's months upon months upon months of groceries. That's months upon months of rent. That might be your entire annual deductible if you've got private health insurance coverage," Gov. Andy Beshear said.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, NewsNation

item.title: White House invites Rachel Morin's mother to press briefing to share daughter's story
item.description: The mother of a Maryland woman who was killed by an individual who entered the country illegally addressed reporters at the White House on Wednesday as the Trump administration seeks to go on offense over the case of an individual who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs seeks two-month delay of May 5 trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Attorneys for hip-hop producer Sean “Diddy” Combs asked a federal judge in New York on Wednesday to delay his May 5 sex-trafficking trial by two months so they can better prepare a defense.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Temu and Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs
item.description: Both companies said they would be making “price adjustments” starting April 25, although neither provided details about the size of the increases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration plans to end the IRS Direct File program for free tax filing, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration plans to eliminate the IRS' Direct File program, an electronic system for filing tax returns directly to the agency for free, according to two people familiar with the decision.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to eliminate habitat protections for endangered and threatened species in a move environmentalists say would lead to the extinction of critically endangered species due to logging, mining, development and other activities.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawyers file wrongful death notice against Idaho city for family of boy shot by police
item.description: A California law firm notified the city of Pocatello, Idaho, on Wednesday of its intention to file a federal wrongful death lawsuit on the behalf of the family of Victor Perez, a 17-year-old boy with intellectual disabilities who was shot multiple times by four police officers.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body is found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete nearly two weeks after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Davido's '5ive' welcomes the Afrobeats megastar into an era of change and growth
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Afrobeats may be the hot sound of the moment, but Davido remembers a very recent past when music from Africa wasn’t embraced globally, amid a perception the continent was one of only destitute poverty and primitive lands.
item.dc:creator: by GARY GERARD HAMILTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 10 guards charged over the fatal beating of a New York inmate, including 2 with murder
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's teases the return of the Snack Wrap
item.description: McDonald's has finally teased a re-release date for a once-popular menu item. Kind of. In a relatively cryptic post to X on Tuesday, the fast-food chain announced the beloved Snack Wraps would return on the 14th.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KTLA 5

item.title: Conservation group names Mississippi River 'most endangered,' cites proposed FEMA cuts
item.description: American Rivers, a nonprofit environmental advocacy group, said the Mississippi River in recent years has faced “increasingly frequent and severe floods” that have damaged homes and businesses and worsened the health of the river, which provides drinking water for 20 million people.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with El Salvador's vice president in push for Abrego Garcia's release
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen traveled to El Salvador on Wednesday and met with the country’s vice president to push for the release of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a man who was sent there by the Trump administration in March despite an immigration court order preventing his deportation.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: MrBeast fans seek refunds after Las Vegas event
item.description: A crowd of frustrated "MrBeast" fans gathered Tuesday in Las Vegas to demand refunds for a three-day experience.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, KRON4

item.title: 9 jurors picked as Weinstein’s lawyers seek to move him from jail to hospital during #MeToo retrial
item.description: Nine jurors have been picked on the second day of jury selection in Harvey Weinstein’s #MeToo rape retrial in New York.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An Ethiopian church in Washington preserves an ancient language and Orthodox tradition
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the U.S. capital, hundreds of Ethiopians dressed in traditional white shawls chant in the ancient liturgical language of one of the oldest branches of Christianity. During hourslong worship services, the Ge’ez language reverberates throughout DSK Mariam Church.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DJ Daniel sworn in as honorary officer
item.description: The Berkeley Police Department hosted over 200 departments so that DJ Daniel could be sworn in as an honorary officer.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ announces civil suit against Maine over transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Tuesday announced a civil lawsuit against Maine’s Department of Education for violating Title IX, the federal law against sex discrimination that the Trump administration has said prohibits transgender students from participating in girls’ and women’s sports in schools.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man in tactical gear attacks officers with pepper spray at Boston-area courthouse, video shows
item.description: Police in a Boston suburb have arrested a man who they say entered a courthouse while wearing a gas mask and tactical gear and attacked several people with pepper spray.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Children on playgrounds targeted by drug runners with candy-colored 'trash cans,' DEA agent warns
item.description: Some drug dealers are moving toward selling drugs in small, brightly colored capsules called "trash cans." The containers have been found near schools and on playgrounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in shooting at Dallas high school is in custody, officials say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A suspect in a shooting at a Dallas high school that wounded four students and drew a heavy police response to the campus has been taken into custody, school district officials announced Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man charged with arson at Pennsylvania governor's home struggled with mental health, brother says
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man accused of setting fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s mansion over the weekend had struggled with mental illness, twice being treated at a psychiatric hospital, as his life unraveled dramatically in the past few years, his brother said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FL representative says he'll go to El Salvador if wrongly deported man isn't returned this week
item.description: U.S. House Representative Maxwell Frost (D-Fla.) says he'll join other Democrat lawmakers planning to go to El Salvador if a man mistakenly deported isn't returned this week.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: CBP officers seize over $14M of alleged methamphetamine at southern border
item.description: CBP officers working at the Pharr International Bridge at the southern border of Texas discovered over $14 million worth of methamphetamine in a shipment of peppers and cucumbers
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend next weekend's White House Correspondents' dinner, The Hill confirmed on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida man confronts neighbor accused of shooting protected bird, video shows
item.description: A heated confrontation between a Florida homeowner and his neighbor over the shooting of a protected bird has prompted outrage in the community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Jeffries: Court should hold Trump officials in contempt over wrongly deported Maryland man
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Tuesday that the Supreme Court should hold members of the Trump administration in contempt of court if they don’t move to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported last month to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, ABC 4

item.title: 5.2 magnitude earthquake strikes near San Diego
item.description: A powerful 5.2 magnitude earthquake rattled the San Diego County area Monday morning. According to the U.S. Geological Survey, the quake struck at 10:08 a.m. The epicenter was about 2.5 miles South of Julian, at a depth of roughly 8 miles.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump, Bukele say they won't return mistakenly deported man to US
item.description: Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele said Monday that he has no plans to return a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in his country, telling reporters, “Of course I’m not going to do it.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The hierarchy behind the Hawaiian shirts at Trader Joe's
item.description: If you’re shopping at Trader Joe’s and you need the kind of assistance that only a seasoned, veteran TJ’s employee can provide, you might want to look at their shirts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5
